title: Pool config extension query has no timeout or response schema
reasoning: POOL_CONFIG_EXT is an isolated network query used specifically to survive schema rollout/resync windows, but this component calls useGQL without the available timeout/schema options. If Hasura stalls, the request can remain wedged instead of failing open on the reward tile; if the response shape drifts, the component silently treats it as missing data and renders an em dash rather than surfacing a typed query error. The linked test mock discards all useGQL arguments, so it would not catch either missing option or a future regression in the query variables/options contract.
recommendation: Import the shared Hasura timeout and POOL_CONFIG_EXT response schema, then call useGQL with an options object such as { timeoutMs: HASURA_TIMEOUT_MS, schema: PoolConfigExtSchema }. Update the test mock to forward and assert the query, variables, and options for the reward query.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The pool-config-panel tests only return canned data from a zero-argument useGQL mock, so they verify rendered output but not the actual query contract or resilience options.
suggestedRegressionTest: In pool-config-panel.test.tsx, make the useGQL mock capture its arguments and assert PoolConfigPanel calls useGQL with POOL_CONFIG_EXT, { id, chainId }, and options containing a timeout plus the config extension schema.

history.note: PoolConfigPanel now passes HASURA_TIMEOUT_MS and PoolConfigExtSchema to the isolated POOL_CONFIG_EXT useGQL call, with focused assertion coverage.
